summary_one_line: A Kannada-script syllable spanning at least 3 glyphs (start+3<=end), reached by long repeated multi-glyph byte runs.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A KANNADA-script buffer whose current syllable spans >=3 glyphs so that start+3<=end holds; produced by long repeated multi-byte glyph runs (e.g. runs of 0x74 'tttttttt' and repeated 'cb 0c 00 00' structures) that decode into syllables of length >=3.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves the branch in all 10 trials with avg hitcount 52; its winning seeds carry long homogeneous runs (Seed 3/5 'ssssssssss', Seed 1 'tttttttt') that produce multi-glyph syllables, and naive's pure havoc byte-insert/copy mutators grow input length unconstrained, frequently producing >=3-glyph syllables.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks in 8/10 trials with avg hitcount 0; at the blocker line L479 the loser takes True 0 times (T=0 F=15) while still reaching the Kannada script check at L478 (T=15), i.e. it enters Kannada syllables but they are never long enough; cmplog's loser seeds are short (10-60 bytes) and its I2S substitution pins bytes to intercepted CMP operands rather than lengthening the input.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive wins by pure havoc length-growth; this is a branch where the I2S technique HURTS: cmplog (loser) spends mutation budget on I2SRandReplace operand substitutions that do not increase syllable length, while naive's unconstrained byte-insert/copy havoc readily reaches start+3<=end. The differentiating winner mechanism is havoc-only mutation, not comparison solving.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a KANNADA-script syllable spanning >=3 glyphs (start+3<=end). The axis distinguishing the two fuzzers is the value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient) feedback, not the byte feature itself.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 8/10 trials (avg hitcount 2) vs cmplog's 1/10; its winning seeds (Seed 1/2, vpc trial 1) carry the same long glyph runs ('tttttttt', repeated 'G 0c 00 00') that build >=3-glyph syllables, and vpc adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward progress toward branch-relevant comparison states cmplog cannot see.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser in this pair too) blocks in 8/10 trials and never takes the True side at L479 (T=0 F=15); lacking the CMP_MAP gradient feedback it gets no partial-progress reward for inputs that approach the >=3-glyph syllable length and stays stuck on short inputs.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog wins over cmplog via the CMP_MAP gradient: the Hamming/value-profile comparison feedback gives a corpus-add signal for inputs that make incremental progress toward the comparison/length states gating the branch, feedback that bare cmplog lacks. The differentiating winner technique is the CMP_MAP gradient.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the syllable-span condition start+3<=end, gated on the Kannada script, with the True side globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:       start + 3 <= end &&
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: naive resolves in 10/10 trials and is the winner over cmplog on the I2S delta.
cmplog is the loser with avg hitcount 0 while naive (winner) hits the branch 52 times on average.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=52  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line the loser never takes True while still reaching the Kannada check, showing it enters Kannada syllables that are never long enough.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 479  T=21 F=13  T=0 F=15  start + 3 <= end &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser still reaches the Kannada-script branch (L478 True 15 times), so blocking is the length condition, not the script.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 478  T=34 F=0  T=15 F=0  if (buffer->props.script == HB_SCRIPT_KANNADA &&

evidence_trail.claim: naive winning seeds carry long homogeneous byte runs that decode into multi-glyph syllables.
A vpc winning seed carries a long run of 0x74 building a multi-glyph syllable.
cmplog loser seeds are short (10-60 bytes).
naive's mutator set is pure havoc plus token with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand for another at matching offsets rather than lengthening input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog is the winner over cmplog on the value_profile delta.
value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 vs cmplog 1/10 on this branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=8/10  blocked=2  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=8  unreached=1

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets to the feedback over cmplog.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Neither winning mechanism is I2SRandReplace: in Pair 1 the winner is naive (no I2S, havoc-only) and in Pair 2 the winner is value_profile_cmplog via the CMP_MAP gradient over cmplog; cmplog is the LOSER in both pairs, so the I2S-floor lineage check does not apply to the winners and was not run. The closed-vocab single slot here records the Pair 2 winner technique (CMP_MAP gradient); the Pair 1 winner technique is havoc-only as stated in its hypothesis.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating True only on start+3<=end in a Kannada buffer where cmplog resolves as fast as or faster than naive and value_profile_cmplog, showing I2S/CMP_MAP help rather than hinder reaching a pure length/syllable-span gate.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-to-glyph decoding is inferred: I assert the long 0x74/0x73 runs decode into >=3-glyph Kannada syllables, but the prompt shows only raw bytes and per-line counts, not the actual decoded glyph stream length.
